system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

nu              1e-02;

nuInf           10;
m               0.4;
n               3;

vertices

blocks
(

);

(

boundary
(

        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }
    outlet

        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    top
    {

        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {

        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}')]</foamfiles>

(
Newtonian
constant
)


    From function static Foam::autoPtr<Foam::viscosityModel> Foam::viscosityModel::New(const Foam::fvMesh&, const Foam::word&)
    in file viscosityModels/viscosityModel/viscosityModelNew.C at line 58.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

**Error Message:**
```
Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

Valid viscosity models are :
2
(
Newtonian
constant
)
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is the same error as Attempts 5 and 6. The `blockMesh` is now working (no blockMesh error), so the mesh exists. The only issue is the viscosity model specification in `constant/momentumTransport`.

The `generalizedNewtonian` laminar model in this version of OpenFOAM (Foundation version, likely openfoam.org v2.0 based on the file paths) only recognizes two viscosity models: `Newtonian` and `constant`. The `CrossPowerLaw` model is not available in the `generalizedNewtonian` framework.

**Previous Approaches Tried:**
- Attempt 5: Tried `laminarModel CrossPowerLaw` directly — not recognized.
- Attempt 6: Tried embedding `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` alongside `generalizedNewtonian` — not recognized.
- Attempt 7 (current): Same as Attempt 6 — still using `generalizedNewtonian` with `constant` sub-model.

**New Approach Needed:**

Since the user requirement states **"Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity"** and also states **"use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties"**, the key insight is that the `physicalProperties` file already contains the `CrossPowerLaw` coefficients (`nuInf 10, m 0.4, n 3, nu 1e-02`). 

The solution is to **remove the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper entirely** from `momentumTransport` and instead specify the `CrossPowerLaw` viscosity model directly at the top level of the `laminar` dictionary, allowing it to read the coefficients from `physicalProperties`. Some versions of OpenFOAM allow a viscosity model to be specified independently of the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper.

**Proposed changes to `constant/momentumTransport`:**

Remove the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper and specify the model directly:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;

    CrossPowerLawCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}
```

If that still fails, another approach is to **specify the viscosity model directly without any wrapper**:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;
}
```

And ensure the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` (with `nuInf`, `m`, `n`, `nu`) are present in `physicalProperties` — some versions of OpenFOAM look for the coefficients in `physicalProperties` automatically when the model is specified.

If neither of these work, the final fallback is to use the `constant` model directly (which is what the user also requested as a fallback: "use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02"):

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    constant;

    constantCoeffs
    {
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}
```

**Most Likely Working Solution:** Based on the history of attempts, the `generalizedNewtonian` approach with `constant` sub-model has been tried and the error persists. The next logical step is to **drop the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper entirely** and specify `CrossPowerLaw` directly as the `laminarModel`. This is a different approach from all previous attempts. If that still fails, use `constant` directly.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/momentumTransport', changes='Remove the generalizedNewtonian wrapper; Set laminarModel directly to CrossPowerLaw; Keep CrossPowerLawCoeffs block intact so it reads nuInf, m, n, nu from physicalProperties')]
